pending_context.notes: "He was from out of the bomb"; "Bombingham" was the canonical SNCC/SCLC nickname for Birmingham during the 1963 bombings // [PASS-6: narrowed
"we had to sit up in the cross-ness and the segregated era"; canonical Jim Crow-era nickname for the segregated upper balcony of southern movie theaters
Speaker's natural form for NAG members

"her name was Cynthia Jones, no deceased. She was very pale, very blonde"; the white CORE ally who walked next to Thelwell at Virginia/Maryland demonstrations, much to his anxiety about lynching risk; speaker establishes she has since died

"La Vaughan, I never forget him. He was in a room next door to me. He was from Birmingham. He was a short, wire little black guy who was a veteran"; the Black Birmingham veteran who first told Thelwell about Bull Connor in fall 1959, four years before national fame

pending_context.candidate_correction: they're going to put me on a stand by
pending_context.notes: At SRT line 13167 (03:54:03 segment); the FBI agents had just threatened to deport Thelwell if he were not enrolled by September; "sandbag" appears to be Whisper's substitution for either "stand-by" (visa idiom) or a similar phrase about being re-enrolled. Idiom-level error, not load-bearing for entity identification.

At SRT line 5751 (01:37:04 segment) re Forman's Waveland speech: "the text and substanceage in the speech was the polygiotoe, which you had come, he said, sojournal motor fleets"; possible "peroration" (the closing rhetorical climax of a formal speech, the canonical word for the speech's emotional finish), but also possibly "polyglot oath" given Thelwell's literary background; preserve as common-noun uncertainty.

Promote from low (.34.6) to medium with surrounding-text validation; adversarial flag retained.
